notes: Disabling d3D11 and manual installing the patch talked about here helps get past the launcher and key code problems. Then you meet the final boss, Uplay. Uplay is evil. There does seem to be a path through, but it sounds like it is not the symbolic link solution! Its a problem with libgnutls.so.26. Instead it sounds like you need to make Uplay install Steam and then jump through some more hopes... manually switch some DLLs something like that. Expect a lot of time to be put in to get the thing working.
title: Anno 2070™

Then I created a uplay account. Linked it to the steam account when asked for. The game starts then, but it always tell me that the game addon was updated and it wants to reboot. Can't get past this :-(
title: Anno 2070™

notes: Proton 4.2-1 allows the game to run almost without workarounds. To get past the black launcher window, add the parameter "PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command%". Once the game is installed, you can remove it and it runs fine on DXVK. I had to run the game at least 20 times to get past the "addon installed, game needs reboot" message. But then it worked fine.
title: Anno 2070™

notes: Launching the game is very painful, most of the time autopatcher is failing contacting server or downloading all content. Also, sound is crackling after the intro videos, unplayable. (I'm using alsa, no pulseaudio)
title: Anno 2070™

notes:
Could not get Uplay to open properly. Had some success though. 
1) set PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command% in the launcher options, this will fix the black screen. 
2) press play and the Activator will appear; with Anno 2070 selected in the Steam Client, under LINKS, click CD KEY, press "Copy to clipboard" button, close the CD KEY UI, 
3) wait for the Autopatcher to start (may take a while), if you don't get the warning dialog saying "could not connect to update server", press cancel and try again, autopatcher should open first, again (may take a while), if you get the warning press OK,
4) wait for Uplay patcher to start (may take a while), allow it to complete, if the game crashes after this, start the game again.
5) the Autopatched partially patched the game, then I got a "could not connect to update server warning", then pressed OK.
6) wait for Uplay updater to start, allow to complete (2 stages).
7) then Uplay updater closes, and nothing happens. I can see Uplay.exe running in processes, but the UI never opens, have to "end process" in System Monitor to close it.
title: Anno 2070™
